name: Betazed
subtitle: Iota Cassiopeia sector
desc: Homeworld of the empathic and telepathic Betazoids. Betazed is known for its lush forests and warm climate. During the Dominion War, the planet fell to Dominion forces — a critical strategic blow to the Federation.

events.text: Betazed falls to Dominion occupation after only 10 hours of resistance
Betazed liberated near the end of the Dominion War

name: Breen
subtitle: Breen homeworld · Breen Confederacy
desc: The enigmatic homeworld of the Breen Confederacy. Contrary to Federation intelligence for centuries, Breen is not a frozen world — though the Breen themselves perpetuate this myth. The Breen wear refrigeration suits at all times and their biology remains classified.

events.text: Breen join the Dominion; crippling energy weapon devastates Federation-Klingon fleet
Breen attack on Starfleet Headquarters in San Francisco — first attack on Earth since 2153
Energy dampening weapon countered; Breen forces defeated at Cardassia Prime

name: Cardassia Prime
subtitle: Cardassian system
population: Reduced to ~800 million after the war; formerly ~5 billion
desc: The Cardassian homeworld, a once-proud civilisation brought to near-extinction. In the final hours of the Dominion War, the Founders ordered Jem'Hadar troops to massacre the civilian population after the Cardassian fleet turned against the Dominion. The Cardassian Restoration began in 2376.

events.text: Cardassian occupation of Bajor begins — lasting 50 years of brutal rule
Cardassians withdraw from Bajor; Terok Nor handed to the Federation
Cardassia joins the Dominion; Cardassian fleet and industry placed under Jem'Hadar control
Dominion massacres 800 million Cardassian civilians in the final battle; war ends

name: Denobula
subtitle: Denobula Triaxa system
desc: A remarkably overcrowded homeworld — Denobulan society adapted to extreme population density through communal living and large extended family groups. Denobulans practice polygamous marriage and are unusually cheerful as a species.

events.text: Dr. Phlox joins Enterprise NX-01 as Chief Medical Officer through the Interspecies Medical Exchange

name: Ferenginar
subtitle: Ferengi home system
desc: The perpetually rain-soaked homeworld of the Ferengi. Commerce, profit, and the 285 Rules of Acquisition govern all aspects of Ferengi life. The Grand Nagus rules from the Tower of Commerce. Under Nagus Rom, Ferengi society began progressive reforms including female rights.

events.text: Grand Nagus Zek retires; Rom appointed Grand Nagus, beginning major social reforms

name: Khitomer
subtitle: Khitomer system · Beta Quadrant
desc: Site of both the historic Khitomer Accords (2293) establishing Federation-Klingon peace, and the Romulan massacre of 2346 that killed over 4,000 Klingon colonists. Worf's parents died here; he was raised by Sergey Rozhenko.

events.text: Khitomer Accords signed by the Federation and Klingon Empire
Romulan attack destroys the Klingon colony; 4,000+ killed
Enterprise-D exposes Duras family's treachery in the massacre

name: Narendra III
subtitle: Narendra system
desc: In 2344, the USS Enterprise-C answered a distress call from this Klingon colony under Romulan attack, sacrificing itself. The act of self-sacrifice so moved the Klingons that it forged the lasting Federation-Klingon alliance that held through the Dominion War.

events.text: USS Enterprise-C sacrifices itself defending the colony; Captain Garrett killed; crew captured by Romulans
Enterprise-C emerges through a temporal rift into the present

name: Remus
subtitle: Twin world of Romulus · Eisn system
desc: The tidally locked sister world of Romulus, one hemisphere scorched by Eisn, the other in permanent darkness. Home to the Remans — a subjugated species used as slave labour in dilithium mines and shock troops in battle. Their connection to the Romulan people remains mysterious.

events.text: Shinzon, half-Romulan Reman, seizes the Romulan Senate and attempts to destroy Earth

name: Risa
subtitle: Hoobishan Baths · Epsilon Ceti system
desc: The Federation's premier resort world, with a climate engineered to be tropical year-round via weather modification technology. Risian society is open and welcoming — the Horga'hn statuette signals an openness to Jamaharon.

events.text: Enterprise-D crew vacation disrupted by Vorgon criminals seeking the Tox Uthat
Risa vacation disrupted by a Suliban agent stealing Enterprise's plasma injectors

name: Romulus
subtitle: Eisn (128 Trianguli) · destroyed 2387
desc: The Romulan homeworld for two millennia. A green-hued Class M world orbiting the star Eisn. Destroyed in 2387 when the Hobus star went supernova. Spock's failure to prevent the destruction with red matter triggered a temporal incident creating the Kelvin timeline.

events.text: Romulan Star Empire founded by exiles from Vulcan following the Sundering
Earth-Romulan War ends; Romulan Neutral Zone established without direct communication
Hobus supernova destroys Romulus; billions die; Romulan diaspora begins
Romulan Free State and Romulan Reclamation Site established near the Artifact

name: Sol system
subtitle: Earth · Sector 001
desc: Homeworld of Humanity and the beating heart of the United Federation of Planets. Starfleet Command is in San Francisco, California; the Federation Council meets in Paris. Sol is the most strategically important system in the Alpha Quadrant.

events.text: Zefram Cochrane achieves warp flight; first contact with Vulcans at Bozeman, Montana
United Federation of Planets founded at San Francisco
Borg cube destroyed near Wolf 359 after devastating the fleet
Breen attack on Starfleet Headquarters during the Dominion War

name: Talos IV
subtitle: Talos star group
desc: Forbidden world of the reclusive, big-headed Talosians. Their powerful telepathic abilities create perfect illusions indistinguishable from reality. Starfleet General Order 7 — the only death-penalty order remaining in Federation law — prohibits all contact.

events.text: Captain Christopher Pike held captive by Talosians; the Menagerie incident
Spock returns Pike to Talos IV in violation of General Order 7

name: Tellar Prime
subtitle: 61 Cygni · 11.4 ly from Sol
desc: Homeworld of the Tellarites, stocky pig-snouted humanoids famous for their combative debating culture. Arguing is a form of social greeting in Tellarite society. Founding member of the Federation.

events.text: Tellarite ambassador Gral participates in the Coalition of Planets conference
Tellar Prime signs the Federation Charter
